_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-1 Medication Administration (Revised 3/3/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-1: Medication Administration — MDHHS Approved 3/3/2025
purpose: Govern medication administration by EMS providers. All medication administration must follow the Six Rights. Provider must verify patient identity, correct medication, correct dose, correct route, correct time, and document. Intranasal (IN) administration for MFR is MCA Optional. Nebulized medication administration for EMT is MCA Optional.

sections.title: Six Rights of Medication Administration
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 9-1: Medication Administration — Six Rights
points: 1. Right Patient — verify patient identity before administering
2. Right Medication — confirm correct drug; check label three times (when selecting, preparing, and before administration)
3. Right Dose — confirm correct dose per protocol or online medical control order
4. Right Route — confirm correct administration route per protocol
5. Right Time — confirm timing is appropriate; check for previous doses administered
6. Right Documentation — document all medications administered including drug, dose, route, time, indication, and patient response

sections.title: General Medication Administration Requirements
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 9-1: Medication Administration — General Requirements
points: Check medication for: correct drug name, correct concentration, expiration date, clarity/absence of particulate matter, and intact packaging/vial
Verbally confirm medication name, dose, and route with partner or team member prior to administration when feasible
Document all medications administered in the PCR including: drug name, dose, route, time, indication, and patient response (desired effect, adverse effects)
Report all adverse medication reactions to online medical control and document in PCR

Paramedic scope includes all routes; Specialist scope per MCA approval; EMT scope: oral, auto-injector, nebulized (if MCA authorized), and assisting patient with prescribed medications; MFR scope: oral, auto-injector, and IN (if MCA authorized)

sections.title: Intranasal (IN) Medication Administration
points: Intranasal (IN) route: use mucosal atomizer device (MAD) only
Administer half the dose in each nostril to distribute across mucosal surface
Confirm absence of nasal obstruction, significant epistaxis, or facial trauma that would prevent absorption
MFR use of IN route: MCA OPTIONAL — only authorized if MCA has approved IN medication administration for MFR
EMT and above: IN route permitted per applicable medication protocols without additional MCA selection (unless specific drug requires MCA authorization)

sections.title: Nebulized Medication Administration
points: Nebulized medications: place in nebulizer cup, attach mouthpiece or mask appropriate for patient age and cooperation
Oxygen flow rate: 6-8 LPM to produce adequate aerosol; may use air compressor if oxygen-driven nebulizer not available
Effective treatment period: approximately 8-10 minutes for standard nebulized dose
EMT administration of nebulized medications: MCA OPTIONAL — only authorized if MCA has approved nebulized medication administration for EMT
Specialist and Paramedic: nebulized route permitted per applicable medication protocols

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must authorize MFR providers to administer medications via intranasal route
options: Authorized — MFR may administer intranasal medications per applicable protocols
Not authorized — MFR intranasal administration not permitted

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must authorize EMT providers to administer nebulized medications
options: Authorized — EMT may administer nebulized medications per applicable protocols
Not authorized — EMT nebulized administration not permitted

deterioration_flags: Adverse medication reaction — notify online medical control immediately; document in PCR
Wrong medication or dose administered — notify online medical control immediately; monitor patient closely; document
scope_notes: All EMS provider levels (scope varies by provider level)
Six Rights must be verified before every medication administration
All medications documented: drug name, dose, route, time, indication, patient response
Adverse reactions: notify online MC; document in PCR
